title: Monorail microcatheter
abstract: A catheter (100) including a first tubular structure (102) defining a first lumen (104), the first tubular structure having a first end (106) and a second end (108). The catheter may also include a second tubular structure (110) defining a second lumen (112), the second tubular structure having a first end (114) and a second end (116). The catheter may also include an aperture (118) between the first tubular structure and the second tubular structure. The aperture is located at the first end of the second tubular structure. A first portion of the first tubular structure extends beyond the aperture, and the second tubular structure is positioned adjacent to a second portion of the first tubular structure.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A catheter comprising: a first tubular structure defining a first lumen, the first tubular structure having a first end and a second end; a second tubular structure defining a second lumen, the second tubular structure having a first end and a second end; and an aperture between the first tubular structure and the second tubular structure, the aperture located at the first end of the second tubular structure, wherein a first portion of the first tubular structure extends between the aperture and the first end of the first tubular structure, and wherein a second portion of the first tubular structure is coupled to and along a length of the second tubular structure. 2. The catheter of claim 1, wherein a stiffness of the first portion of the first tubular structure is less than a stiffness of the second portion of the first tubular structure and a stiffness of the second tubular structure. 3. The catheter of claim 1 or 2, wherein the second portion of the first tubular structure and the second tubular structure each comprise a first material, and wherein the first portion of the first tubular structure comprises a second material that is different than the first material. 4. The catheter of any one of claims 1-3, further comprising: a baffle coupled to an interior surface of the first tubular structure, wherein the baffle at least partially obstructs the first lumen of the first tubular structure. 5. The catheter of claim 4, wherein the baffle is rotatably coupled to the interior surface of the first tubular structure. 6. The catheter of any one of claims 1-5, wherein a thickness of the first portion of the first tubular structure is less than a thickness of the second portion of the first tubular structure and a thickness of the second tubular structure. 7. The catheter of any one of claims 1-6, wherein a longitudinal axis of the first lumen is parallel to a longitudinal axis of the second lumen at a given location along the length of the first tubular structure and the second tubular structure. 8. The catheter of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the first end of the second tubular structure comprises a first opening, wherein the second end of the first tubular structure comprises a second opening, and wherein the second end of the second tubular structure comprises a third opening. 9. The catheter of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the first end of the second tubular structure comprises an angled surface arranged to provide an angled transition from the second lumen to the first lumen through the aperture. 10. The catheter of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the first end of the second tubular structure comprises a rounded surface arranged to provide a rounded transition from the second lumen to the first lumen through the aperture. 11. The catheter of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the first portion the first tubular structure is tapered. 12. The catheter of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the first portion of the first tubular structure includes a variable diameter. 13. The catheter of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the catheter includes a plurality of metal strands arranged longitudinally or helically along at least a portion of a length of one or both of the first tubular structure and the second tubular structure. 14. The catheter of claim 13, wherein the plurality of metal strands comprises stainless steel, cobalt chromium, nitinol, or a combination thereof. 15. The catheter of claim 13 or 14, wherein the plurality of metal strands are braided. 16. The catheter of any one of claims 13-15, wherein the plurality of metal strands comprise strips. 17. The catheter of any one of claims 13-16, wherein a portion of the plurality of metal strands arranged along the second portion of the first tubular structure and the second tubular structure are wider than a portion of the plurality of metal strands arranged along the first portion of the first tubular structure. 18. The catheter of any one of claims 13-17, wherein a portion of the plurality of metal strands arranged along the second portion of the first tubular structure and the second tubular structure are thicker than a portion of the plurality of metal strands arranged along the first portion of the first tubular structure. 19. The catheter of any one of claims 1-18, wherein the first lumen has a length ranging from about 30 cm to about 170 cm. 20. The catheter of any one of claims 1-19, wherein a diameter of the second portion of the first tubular structure has a range from about 0.35 mm to about 6 mm. 21. The catheter of any one of claims 1-20, wherein the second tubular structure has a length ranging from about 5 cm to about 160 cm. 22. The catheter of any one of claims 1-21, wherein a diameter of the second tubular structure has a range from about 0.25 mm to about 1.2 mm. 23. The catheter of any one of claims 1-22, wherein the first portion of the first tubular structure has a length ranging from about 3 cm to about 25 cm. 24. The catheter of any one of claims 1-23, wherein a diameter of the first portion of the first tubular structure has a range from about 0.35 mm to about 6 mm. 25. The catheter of any one of claims 1-24, wherein the first portion of the first tubular structure has a radius of curvature ranging from about 0 mm to about 170 mm. 26. A catheter comprising: a tubular structure defining a lumen, the tubular structure having a first end and a second end, wherein the first end of the tubular structure comprises a first opening, and wherein the second end of the tubular structure comprises a second opening; and a wire tip extending from the first end of the tubular structure, wherein the wire tip includes a first portion coupled to the first end of the tubular structure and a second portion extending away from the first portion, wherein a stiffness of the second portion of the wire tip is less than a stiffness of the first portion of the wire tip. 27. The catheter of claim 26, wherein the first portion of the wire tip comprises a first material, and wherein the second portion of wire tip comprises a second material that is different than the first material. 28. The catheter of claim 26 or 27, wherein a thickness of the first portion of the wire tip is greater than a thickness of the second portion of the wire tip. 29. The catheter of any one of claims 26-28, wherein the first end of the tubular structure is tapered. 30. The catheter of any one of claims 26-29, wherein the first end of the tubular structure includes a variable diameter. 31. The catheter of any one of claims 26-30, wherein the lumen of the tubular structure has a length ranging from about 30 cm to about 170 cm. 32. The catheter of any one of claims 26-31, wherein a diameter of the tubular structure has a range from about 0.35 mm to about 6 mm. 33. The catheter of any one of claims 26-32, wherein the wire tip is substantially straight. 34. The catheter of any one of claims 26-33, wherein the first end of the tubular structure has a radius of curvature ranging from about 0 mm to about 170 mm. 35. A method comprising: introducing a catheter according to any one of claims 1 -25 and a guidewire together into an arterial configuration, wherein a distal end of the guidewire extends distal to the first end of the first tubular structure; advancing the catheter and the guidewire together through the arterial configuration until the distal end of the guidewire is advanced across an occlusion in the arterial configuration; advancing the catheter with respect to the guidewire in the arterial configuration such that the first end of the first tubular structure is advanced across the occlusion; retracting the distal end of the guidewire into the first tubular structure through the aperture and into the second tubular structure; advancing a revascularization device through the second end of the first tubular structure to the first end of the first tubular structure; and retracting the catheter relative to the neovascularization device such that the revascularization device is deployed into the arterial configuration with a first end of the revascularization device located distal to the occlusion and a second end of the revascularization device located proximal to the occlusion. 36. The method of claim 35, further comprising: removing the revascularization device from the arterial configuration after a predetermined period of time; and determining whether the occlusion has been removed from the arterial configuration. 37. The method of claim 36, further comprising: in response to a determination that the occlusion has been removed from the arterial configuration, removing the guidewire from the arterial configuration; and removing the catheter from the arterial configuration. 38. The method of claim 36, further comprising: in response to a determination that the occlusion has not been removed from the arterial configuration, re-advancing the distal end of the guidewire through the aperture and into the first lumen of the first tubular structure and exiting the catheter at the first end of the first tubular structure; re-advancing the first end of the first tubular structure across the occlusion over the guidewire such that the first end of the first tubular structure is located distal to the occlusion; advancing a second revascularization device through the catheter, such that the second revascularization device enters the catheter at the second end of the first tubular structure and exits the catheter at the first end of the first tubular structure; 

 introducing the second revascularization device into the arterial configuration such that a first end of the second revascularization device is located distal to the occlusion and a second end of the second revascularization device is located proximal to the occlusion; and removing the second revascularization device from the arterial configuration after a predetermined period of time. 39. The method of claim 36, further comprising: in response to a determination that the occlusion has not been removed from the arterial configuration, re-advancing the distal end of the guidewire through the aperture and into the first lumen of the first tubular structure and exiting the catheter at the first end of the first tubular structure; re-advancing the first end of the first tubular structure across the occlusion over the guidewire such that the first end of the first tubular structure is located distal to the occlusion; re-advancing the revascularization device through the catheter, such that the revascularization device enters the catheter at the second end of the first tubular structure and exits the catheter at the first end of the first tubular structure; introducing the revascularization device into the arterial configuration such that a first end of the revascularization device is located distal to the occlusion and a second end of the revascularization device is located proximal to the occlusion; and removing the revascularization device from the arterial configuration after a predetermined period of time. 40. The method of any one of claims 35-39, further comprising, after retracting the distal end of the guidewire into the first tubular structure through the aperture and into the second tubular structure, retracting the guidewire along with the catheter. 41. The method of any one claims 35-40, wherein the distal end of the guidewire is retracted into the second tubular structure at least a distance corresponding to a length of the occlusion. 42. The method of any one of claims 35-36 and 38-41, wherein retracting the distal end of the guidewire into the first tubular structure through the aperture and into the second tubular structure further comprises retracting the distal end of the guidewire out of the second end of the second tubular structure and into the arterial configuration. 43. The method of claim 42, further comprising: re-advancing the distal end of the guidewire into the second end of the second tubular structure. 44. The method of any one of claims 35-43, wherein the catheter includes a locking device configured to prevent the catheter from moving with respect to the guidewire in a locked mode, and wherein the locking device is configured to allow the catheter to move along the guidewire in an unlocked mode, the method further comprising: 

 placing the locking device in the locked mode prior to introducing the catheter and the guidewire together into the arterial configuration; and placing the locking device in the unlocked mode prior to advancing the catheter with respect to the guidewire in the arterial configuration such that the first end of the first tubular structure is advanced across the occlusion. 45. A method comprising: introducing a guidewire into an arterial configuration and through an occlusion via arterial access; loading the catheter according to any one of claims 1-25 onto the guidewire, such that a proximal end of the guidewire enters the catheter at the first end of the first tubular structure, advances past the aperture and into the second portion of the first tubular structure and exits at the second end of the first tubular structure; advancing the catheter along the guidewire and introducing the catheter into the arterial configuration such that the first end of the first tubular structure is advanced across the occlusion; advancing a revascularization device through the second end of the second tubular structure, through the aperture, and into the first end of the first tubular structure; and retracting the catheter relative to the neovascularization device such that the revascularization device is deployed into the arterial configuration with a first end of the revascularization device located distal to the occlusion and a second end of the revascularization device located proximal to the occlusion. 46. A method comprising: introducing a guidewire into an arterial configuration and through an occlusion via arterial access; loading the catheter according to any one of claims 1-25 onto the guidewire, such that a proximal end of the guidewire enters the catheter at the first end of the first tubular structure, advances through the aperture and into the second lumen of the second tubular structure and exits at the second end of the second tubular structure; moving the catheter along the guidewire and introducing the catheter into the arterial configuration such that the first end of the first tubular structure is advanced across the occlusion; advancing a revascularization device through the second end of the first tubular structure to the first end of the first tubular structure; and retracting the catheter relative to the neovascularization device such that the revascularization device is deployed into the arterial configuration with a first end of the revascularization device located distal to the occlusion and a second end of the revascularization device located proximal to the occlusion. 47. A method comprising: introducing a catheter according to any one of claims 26-34 into an arterial configuration such that the wire tip is advanced across an occlusion, wherein a first end of the first portion of the wire tip is located distal to the occlusion and a second end of the first 

 portion of the wire tip is located proximal to the occlusion when the wire tip is advanced across the occlusion; advancing a revascularization device through the second end of the tubular structure to the first end of the first tubular structure; and advancing the revascularization device across the occlusion such that the revascularization device is deployed into the arterial configuration with a first end of the revascularization device located distal to the occlusion and a second end of the revascularization device located proximal to the occlusion.
